column1: # CPU Credits: sufficient (burst mode active throughout test)
# Warm-up: 2 iterations discarded per request type before recording
# (~1.6–1.8 × 10⁶ instr/ms) due to JIT warmup overhead dominating
# the short execution time. The JIT compiler has not yet fully optimized
# hot loops for these brief workloads.
# Large GrayScott requests (128×2000 and above) show stable throughput
# at ~2.1–2.16 × 10⁶ instr/ms. At this scale the JIT is fully warm
# All measurements were taken with sufficient CPU credits available
# (burst mode). The t3.micro baseline is 10% of a vCPU.
# drops to approximately 0.2 × 10⁶ instr/ms (10% of burst).
# Production auto-scaling policies should account for credit
# Ratio: t3.micro operates at approximately 40–50% of local performance.
# This is consistent with the t3.micro's Intel Xeon Platinum 8000-series
# vCPU running at ~2.5 GHz burst vs a modern desktop at 4+ GHz.
#   This validates the current value of 50
#   Target: scale up when each worker has > 2.5 s of pending work
#   Wall-clock interpretation: if a new request arrives and each
#   existing worker already has ≥ 2.5 s of queued computation
#   a new instance should be launched.
#   Target: scale down when each worker has < 0.6 s of pending work
#   Wall-clock interpretation: if each worker has less than 0.6 s
# Instance i-0a3b7c9d2e4f terminated after test completion.
